user_message: Can you extract the account health from this exec meeting notes for Azure Ridge Partners?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 15:31 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 14:39 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 17:26 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Azure Ridge Partners

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Azure Ridge Partners: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-D trap (Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-D failure mode (Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-D -- Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately
